name: sleuthkit
title: sleuthkit
summary: tools for forensics analysis on volume and filesystem data
description:
tools for forensics analysis on volume and filesystem data
The Sleuth Kit, also known as TSK, is a collection of UNIX-based command
line file and volume system forensic analysis tools. The filesystem tools
allow you to examine filesystems of a suspect computer in a non-intrusive
fashion. Because the tools do not rely on the operating system to process the
filesystems, deleted and hidden content is shown.
.
The volume system (media management) tools allow you to examine the layout of
disks and other media. You can also recover deleted files, get information
stored in slack spaces, examine filesystems journal, see partitions layout on
disks or images etc. But is very important clarify that the TSK acts over the
current filesystem only.
.
The Sleuth Kit supports DOS partitions, BSD partitions (disk labels), Mac
partitions, Sun slices (Volume Table of Contents), and GPT disks. With these
tools, you can identify where partitions are located and extract them so that
they can be analyzed with filesystem analysis tools.
.
Currently, TSK supports several filesystems, as NTFS, FAT, exFAT, HFS+, Ext3,
Ext4, UFS and YAFFS2.
.
This package contains the set of command line tools in The Sleuth Kit.

commands.name: blkcalc
commands.description: Converts between unallocated disk unit numbers and regular disk unit numbers.

commands.name: blkcat
commands.description: Display the contents of file system data unit in a disk image.

commands.name: blkls
commands.description: List or output file system data units.

commands.name: blkstat
commands.description: Display details of a file system data unit (i.e. block or sector)

commands.name: fcat
commands.description: Output the contents of a file based on its name.

commands.name: ffind
commands.description: Finds the name of the file or directory using a given inode

commands.name: fiwalk
commands.description: print the filesystem statistics and exit

commands.name: fls
commands.description: List file and directory names in a disk image.

commands.name: fsstat
commands.description: Display general details of a file system

commands.name: hfind
commands.description: Lookup a hash value in a hash database

commands.name: icat
commands.description: Output the contents of a file based on its inode number.

commands.name: ifind
commands.description: Find the meta-data structure that has allocated a given disk unit or file name.

commands.name: ils
commands.description: List inode information

commands.name: img_cat
commands.description: Output contents of an image file.

commands.name: img_stat
commands.description: Display details of an image file

commands.name: istat
commands.description: Display details of a meta-data structure (i.e. inode)

commands.name: jcat
commands.description: Show the contents of a block in the file system journal.

commands.name: jls
commands.description: List the contents of a file system journal

commands.name: jpeg_extract
commands.description: jpeg extractor

commands.name: mactime
commands.description: Create an ASCII time line of file activity

commands.name: mmcat
commands.description: Output the contents of a partition to stdout

commands.name: mmls
commands.description: Display the partition layout of a volume system (partition tables)

commands.name: mmstat
commands.description: Display details about the volume system (partition tables)

commands.name: pstat
commands.description: Binary from sleuthkit

commands.name: sigfind
commands.description: Find a binary signature in a file

commands.name: sorter
commands.description: Sort files in an image into categories based on file type

commands.name: srch_strings
commands.description: display printable strings in files

commands.name: tsk_comparedir
commands.description: compare the contents of a directory with the contents of an image or local device.

commands.name: tsk_gettimes
commands.description: Collect MAC times from a disk image into a body file.

commands.name: tsk_imageinfo
commands.description: Binary from sleuthkit

commands.name: tsk_loaddb
commands.description: populate a SQLite database with metadata from a disk image

commands.name: tsk_recover
commands.description: Export files from an image into a local directory

commands.name: usnjls
commands.description: List the contents of a NTFS Update Sequence Number journal
